summary: PR #2750 improves the gm_1BFA match for gm_801BFCFC by changing nearby local declaration and helper signature/source shape in src/melee/gm/gm_1BFA.c and aligning struct/member types plus header visibility in src/melee/gm/gm_1BFA.h. The PR body reports no intended behavior change and verification via ninja report plus objdiff improvement to about 99.19%.

key_files.role: Adjusted file comment placement, made gm_801BFC60 static with revised parameter/return types, removed uncertain return-type remark, simplified return cast, and changed several gm_801BFCFC local variable types.
Adjusted un_804A1F48_t member types and removed the public prototype for gm_801BFC60 after the helper became static in the C file.

what_changed.text: Moved the file-level comment from a @remarks block after includes to an @file comment at the top: 'Seems to deal with the challenger approaching functions.'
Changed gm_801BFC60 from an externally declared s32** helper taking s16/s32/s16/s32/void** to a static helper returning UNK_T* and taking u32/s32/u32/u32/UNK_T*.
Removed a remark saying the gm_801BFC60 return type was uncertain and removed an explicit cast in its fallback return path.
Adjusted gm_801BFCFC locals: temp_r3 changed from s32** to UNK_T*, var_r25 from u32 to int, and var_r31 from void** to UNK_T*.
Changed un_804A1F48_t fields x0 and x2 from signed s16 to unsigned u16, and x8 from s32* to UNK_T.

Reported verification: ninja build/GALE01/report.json and objdiff-cli show gm_801BFCFC at 99.1875% with 20 arg mismatches; bot report records gm_801BFCFC improving from 95.34% to 99.19% for main/melee/gm/gm_1BFA.

smart_moves.text: Kept the matching change narrow: only the local helper declaration/source shape, local variable declarations, and related header type/prototype surface changed.
Made gm_801BFC60 static and removed its header declaration, aligning visibility with its local helper role as represented by this PR.
Used UNK_T/UNK_T* to represent not-yet-understood data instead of preserving incompatible-looking s32** or void** shapes.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: gm_1BFA.c gm_801BFC60 and gm_801BFCFC type changes

decomp_lessons.text: For matching-sensitive helper calls, local declaration shape can affect current codegen enough to improve a caller match without intended behavior changes.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: 'adding the local declaration source shape needed for the current codegen'

decomp_lessons.text: When a helper is only needed locally, making it static and removing its public header prototype can be part of the matching/source-shape adjustment, if the diff supports that visibility.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: gm_801BFC60 prototype removed from gm_1BFA.h

naming_conventions.text: No semantic rename was introduced for unknown symbols; address-style names such as gm_801BFC60, gm_801BFCFC, gm_804D6860, and un_804A1F48_t were retained.
The file comment uses tentative wording ('Seems to deal with the challenger approaching functions') rather than asserting an unsupported subsystem name.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.text: Changed local declarations and helper signature types to influence gm_801BFCFC codegen; PR body explicitly identifies this as the source-shape needed for the current codegen.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: gm_801BFC60 and gm_801BFCFC declaration changes

assembly_or_matching_tactics.text: A pre-existing #pragma push / #pragma dont_inline on / #pragma pop surrounds gm_801BFC60 and was retained while the helper signature changed; the PR slice does not show this pragma being newly added.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: gm_801BFC60 hunk shows pragma context without added pragma lines

assembly_or_matching_tactics.text: Verification evidence reports a match-score improvement for gm_801BFCFC, but not a full match: objdiff-cli 99.1875% with 20 arg mismatches; bot table 95.34% before to 99.19% after.

review_feedback.text: No human review comments were present in the loaded slice.
Automated bot feedback recorded one unmatched-item improvement for main/melee/gm/gm_1BFA / gm_801BFCFC.

evidence_refs: metadata_json: PR metadata, changed files, merge state
raw_diff: complete diff excerpt for two changed files
notes: Evidence is strong for what changed and the reported match improvement.
There are no human review comments, so reviewer intent or acceptance rationale beyond merge status is not available.
The slice supports that local declaration shape improved codegen for this function, but does not prove a broader project-wide rule about using UNK_T or static helpers.
The retained dont_inline pragma is visible only as context in the diff; the PR did not add it, so it should not be treated as a new tactic justified by this PR alone.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Local declaration shape can affect caller codegen
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR #2750, changing gm_801BFC60's local helper signature/visibility and related gm_801BFCFC local declarations was described by the author as adding the local declaration source shape needed for current codegen, and the bot report showed gm_801BFCFC improving from 95.34% to 99.19%. Keep this lesson scoped to gm_1BFA-style matching work unless corroborated elsewhere.

source_update_candidates.title: gm_1BFA tentative challenger-approaching scope
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2750 added a file-level comment to src/melee/gm/gm_1BFA.c: 'Seems to deal with the challenger approaching functions.' This is tentative wording from the diff and should remain scoped/qualified unless other source evidence confirms it.
source_update_candidates.reason: Could help future path-context search, but the wording itself is uncertain and should not be promoted as a definitive subsystem fact without corroboration.

source_update_candidates.title: Keep local helper visibility and declaration shape aligned with codegen evidence
source_update_candidates.text: Consider a standards note that, when a helper is truly local and matching evidence supports it, static visibility plus the local declaration/source shape can be part of the matching hypothesis; header prototypes should be removed only when the helper is not public.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: gm_801BFC60 became static and prototype was removed
source_update_candidates.reason: The PR provides a concrete example, but it is only one small slice; curator should decide whether it adds anything beyond existing truthful-headers and infer-authored-source-style standards.

rejection_notes: Do not infer that UNK_T is generally preferable to recovered concrete types; this PR only shows UNK_T used in this local matching adjustment.
Do not infer that #pragma dont_inline is newly justified by this PR; the pragma appears as retained context, not an added change.
Do not claim gm_801BFCFC fully matched; the reported state remains 99.1875% with 20 arg mismatches.
